story: In a crowded mall, Mary follows a woodsy cologne in Dillard’s that hurls her back to age twelve: chasing her runaway dachshund, crashing into a boy named Callum, learning to ride his bike, lemonade on a porch, and a pinky promise before his family moved away—no phones, no email. A clerk offers a bottle called “Empowered Teakwood.” Mary laughs and leaves without buying. Across town, a chalkboard outside the riverside park café reads: “Welcome back, Callum — sunset ride tonight.” Outside the mall, Mary breathes in the cool air and thinks, Maybe I’ll ride my bike when I get home.
implausible_knowledge: Across town, a chalkboard outside the riverside park café reads: “Welcome back, Callum — sunset ride tonight.”
question: After stepping outside the mall and considering a bike ride, what will Mary most plausibly do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Head to the riverside park to try to meet Callum at the sunset ride.
answer_without_knowledge: Go home and take a bike ride.
